NKJV.1: Now I saw when the Lamb opened one of the seals; and I heard one of the four living creatures saying with a voice like thunder, “Come and see.”
NKJV.2: And I looked, and behold, a white horse. He who sat on it had a bow; and a crown was given to him, and he went out conquering and to conquer.
NKJV.3: When He opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature saying, “Come and see.”
NKJV.4: Another horse, fiery red, went out. And it was granted to the one who sat on it to take peace from the earth, and that people should kill one another; and there was given to him a great sword.
NKJV.5: When He opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, “Come and see.” So I looked, and behold, a black horse, and he who sat on it had a pair of scales in his hand.
NKJV.6: And I heard a voice in the midst of the four living creatures saying, “A quart of wheat for a denarius, and three quarts of barley for a denarius; and do not harm the oil and the wine.”
NKJV.7: When He opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature saying, “Come and see.”
NKJV.8: So I looked, and behold, a pale horse. And the name of him who sat on it was Death, and Hades followed with him. And power was given to them over a fourth of the earth, to kill with sword, with hunger, with death, and by the beasts of the earth.
NKJV.9: When He opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of those who had been slain for the word of God and for the testimony which they held.
NKJV.10: And they cried with a loud voice, saying, “How long, O Lord, holy and true, until You judge and avenge our blood on those who dwell on the earth?”
NKJV.11: Then a white robe was given to each of them; and it was said to them that they should rest a little while longer, until both the number of their fellow servants and their brethren, who would be killed as they were, was completed.
NKJV.12: I looked when He opened the sixth seal, and behold, there was a great earthquake; and the sun became black as sackcloth of hair, and the moon became like blood.
NKJV.13: And the stars of heaven fell to the earth, as a fig tree drops its late figs when it is shaken by a mighty wind.
NKJV.14: Then the sky receded as a scroll when it is rolled up, and every mountain and island was moved out of its place.
NKJV.15: And the kings of the earth, the great men, the rich men, the commanders, the mighty men, every slave and every free man, hid themselves in the caves and in the rocks of the mountains,
NKJV.16: and said to the mountains and rocks, “Fall on us and hide us from the face of Him who sits on the throne and from the wrath of the Lamb!
NKJV.17: For the great day of His wrath has come, and who is able to stand?”

ESV.1: Now I watched when the Lamb opened one of the seven seals, and I heard one of the four living creatures say with a voice like thunder, “Come!”
ESV.2: And I looked, and behold, a white horse! And its rider had a bow, and a crown was given to him, and he came out conquering, and to conquer.
ESV.3: When he opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, “Come!”
ESV.4: And out came another horse, bright red. Its rider was permitted to take peace from the earth, so that people should slay one another, and he was given a great sword.
ESV.5: When he opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, “Come!” And I looked, and behold, a black horse! And its rider had a pair of scales in his hand.
ESV.6: And I heard what seemed to be a voice in the midst of the four living creatures, saying, “A quart  of wheat for a denarius,  and three quarts of barley for a denarius, and do not harm the oil and wine!”
ESV.7: When he opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, “Come!”
ESV.8: And I looked, and behold, a pale horse! And its rider's name was Death, and Hades followed him. And they were given authority over a fourth of the earth, to kill with sword and with famine and with pestilence and by wild beasts of the earth.
ESV.9: When he opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of those who had been slain for the word of God and for the witness they had borne.
ESV.10: They cried out with a loud voice, “O Sovereign Lord, holy and true, how long before you will judge and avenge our blood on those who dwell on the earth?”
ESV.11: Then they were each given a white robe and told to rest a little longer, until the number of their fellow servants and their brothers  should be complete, who were to be killed as they themselves had been.
ESV.12: When he opened the sixth seal, I looked, and behold, there was a great earthquake, and the sun became black as sackcloth, the full moon became like blood,
ESV.13: and the stars of the sky fell to the earth as the fig tree sheds its winter fruit when shaken by a gale.
ESV.14: The sky vanished like a scroll that is being rolled up, and every mountain and island was removed from its place.
ESV.15: Then the kings of the earth and the great ones and the generals and the rich and the powerful, and everyone, slave  and free, hid themselves in the caves and among the rocks of the mountains,
ESV.16: calling to the mountains and rocks, “Fall on us and hide us from the face of him who is seated on the throne, and from the wrath of the Lamb,
ESV.17: for the great day of their wrath has come, and who can stand?”

NASB.1: Then I saw when the Lamb broke one of the seven seals, and I heard one of the four living creatures saying as with a voice of thunder, 'Come.'
NASB.2: I looked, and behold, a white horse, and he who sat on it had a bow; and a crown was given to him, and he went out conquering and to conquer.
NASB.3: When He broke the second seal, I heard the second living creature saying, 'Come.'
NASB.4: And another, a red horse, went out; and to him who sat on it, it was granted to take peace from the earth, and that [men] would slay one another; and a great sword was given to him.
NASB.5: When He broke the third seal, I heard the third living creature saying, 'Come.' I looked, and behold, a black horse; and he who sat on it had a pair of scales in his hand.
NASB.6: And I heard [something] like a voice in the center of the four living creatures saying, 'A quart of wheat for a denarius, and three quarts of barley for a denarius; and do not damage the oil and the wine.'
NASB.7: When the Lamb broke the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature saying, 'Come.'
NASB.8: I looked, and behold, an ashen horse; and he who sat on it had the name Death; and Hades was following with him. Authority was given to them over a fourth of the earth, to kill with sword and with famine and with pestilence and by the wild beasts of the earth.
NASB.9: When the Lamb broke the fifth seal, I saw underneath the altar the souls of those who had been slain because of the word of God, and because of the testimony which they had maintained;
NASB.10: and they cried out with a loud voice, saying, 'How long, O Lord, holy and true, will You refrain from judging and avenging our blood on those who dwell on the earth?'
NASB.11: And there was given to each of them a white robe; and they were told that they should rest for a little while longer, until [the number of] their fellow servants and their brethren who were to be killed even as they had been, would be completed also.
NASB.12: I looked when He broke the sixth seal, and there was a great earthquake; and the sun became black as sackcloth [made] of hair, and the whole moon became like blood;
NASB.13: and the stars of the sky fell to the earth, as a fig tree casts its unripe figs when shaken by a great wind.
NASB.14: The sky was split apart like a scroll when it is rolled up, and every mountain and island were moved out of their places.
NASB.15: Then the kings of the earth and the great men and the commanders and the rich and the strong and every slave and free man hid themselves in the caves and among the rocks of the mountains;
NASB.16: and they said to the mountains and to the rocks, 'Fall on us and hide us from the presence of Him who sits on the throne, and from the wrath of the Lamb;
NASB.17: for the great day of their wrath has come, and who is able to stand?'

NLT.1: As I watched, the Lamb broke the first of the seven seals on the scroll. Then I heard one of the four living beings say with a voice like thunder, “Come!”
NLT.2: I looked up and saw a white horse standing there. Its rider carried a bow, and a crown was placed on his head. He rode out to win many battles and gain the victory.
NLT.3: When the Lamb broke the second seal, I heard the second living being say, “Come!”
NLT.4: Then another horse appeared, a red one. Its rider was given a mighty sword and the authority to take peace from the earth. And there was war and slaughter everywhere.
NLT.5: When the Lamb broke the third seal, I heard the third living being say, “Come!” I looked up and saw a black horse, and its rider was holding a pair of scales in his hand.
NLT.6: And I heard a voice from among the four living beings say, “A loaf of wheat bread or three loaves of barley will cost a day’s pay. And don’t waste the olive oil and wine.”
NLT.7: When the Lamb broke the fourth seal, I heard the fourth living being say, “Come!”
NLT.8: I looked up and saw a horse whose color was pale green. Its rider was named Death, and his companion was the Grave. These two were given authority over one-fourth of the earth, to kill with the sword and famine and disease and wild animals.
NLT.9: When the Lamb broke the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of all who had been martyred for the word of God and for being faithful in their testimony.
NLT.10: They shouted to the Lord and said, “O Sovereign Lord, holy and true, how long before you judge the people who belong to this world and avenge our blood for what they have done to us?”
NLT.11: Then a white robe was given to each of them. And they were told to rest a little longer until the full number of their brothers and sisters — their fellow servants of Jesus who were to be martyred — had joined them.
NLT.12: I watched as the Lamb broke the sixth seal, and there was a great earthquake. The sun became as dark as black cloth, and the moon became as red as blood.
NLT.13: Then the stars of the sky fell to the earth like green figs falling from a tree shaken by a strong wind.
NLT.14: The sky was rolled up like a scroll, and all of the mountains and islands were moved from their places.
NLT.15: Then everyone — the kings of the earth, the rulers, the generals, the wealthy, the powerful, and every slave and free person — all hid themselves in the caves and among the rocks of the mountains.
NLT.16: And they cried to the mountains and the rocks, “Fall on us and hide us from the face of the one who sits on the throne and from the wrath of the Lamb.
NLT.17: For the great day of their wrath has come, and who is able to survive?”

WEB.1: I saw that the Lamb opened one of the seven seals, and I heard one of the four living creatures saying, as with a voice of thunder, “Come and see!”
WEB.2: And behold, a white horse, and he who sat on it had a bow. A crown was given to him, and he came out conquering, and to conquer.
WEB.3: When he opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature saying, “Come!”
WEB.4: Another came out, a red horse. To him who sat on it was given power to take peace from the earth, and that they should kill one another. There was given to him a great sword.
WEB.5: When he opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature saying, “Come and see!” And behold, a black horse, and he who sat on it had a balance in his hand.
WEB.6: I heard a voice in the middle of the four living creatures saying, “A choenix  of wheat for a denarius, and three choenix of barley for a denarius! Don’t damage the oil and the wine!”
WEB.7: When he opened the fourth seal, I heard the fourth living creature saying, “Come and see!”
WEB.8: And behold, a pale horse, and he who sat on it, his name was Death. Hades  followed with him. Authority over one fourth of the earth, to kill with the sword, with famine, with death, and by the wild animals of the earth was given to him.
WEB.9: When he opened the fifth seal, I saw underneath the altar the souls of those who had been killed for the Word of God, and for the testimony of the Lamb which they had.
WEB.10: They cried with a loud voice, saying, “How long, Master, the holy and true, until you judge and avenge our blood on those who dwell on the earth?”
WEB.11: A long white robe was given to each of them. They were told that they should rest yet for a while, until their fellow servants and their brothers,  who would also be killed even as they were, should complete their course.
WEB.12: I saw when he opened the sixth seal, and there was a great earthquake. The sun became black as sackcloth made of hair, and the whole moon became as blood.
WEB.13: The stars of the sky fell to the earth, like a fig tree dropping its unripe figs when it is shaken by a great wind.
WEB.14: The sky was removed like a scroll when it is rolled up. Every mountain and island were moved out of their places.
WEB.15: The kings of the earth, the princes, the commanding officers, the rich, the strong, and every slave and free person, hid themselves in the caves and in the rocks of the mountains.
WEB.16: They told the mountains and the rocks, “Fall on us, and hide us from the face of him who sits on the throne, and from the wrath of the Lamb,
WEB.17: for the great day of his wrath has come; and who is able to stand?”

CSB17.1: Then I saw the Lamb open one of the seven <sup>[1]</sup> seals, and I heard one of the four living creatures say with a voice like thunder, <sup>ⓛ</sup> “Come!”
CSB17.2: I looked, and there was a white horse. <sup>ⓜ</sup> Its rider held a bow; <sup>ⓝ</sup> a crown <sup>ⓐ</sup> was given to him, and he went out as a conqueror in order to conquer. <sup>[2]</sup>
CSB17.3: When he opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, “Come!”
CSB17.4: Then another horse went out, a fiery red one, <sup>ⓑ</sup> and its rider was allowed to take peace from the earth, so that people would slaughter one another. And a large sword was given to him. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.5: When he opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, “Come!” And I looked, and there was a black horse. <sup>ⓓ</sup> Its rider held a set of scales in his hand. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.6: Then I heard something like a voice among the four living creatures say, “A quart of wheat for a denarius, <sup>[3]</sup> and three quarts of barley for a denarius, <sup>ⓕ</sup> but do not harm the oil and the wine.” <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.7: When he opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, “Come!”
CSB17.8: And I looked, and there was a pale green <sup>[4]</sup> horse. Its rider was named Death, and Hades was following after him. <sup>ⓗ</sup> They were <sup>[5]</sup> given authority <sup>ⓘ</sup> over a fourth of the earth, to kill by the sword, by famine, by plague, and by the wild animals of the earth. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.9: When he opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of those who had been slaughtered because of the word of God and the testimony they had given. <sup>[6]</sup> <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.10: They cried out with a loud voice: “Lord, <sup>[7]</sup> the one who is holy and true, <sup>ⓛ</sup> how long until you judge those who live on the earth and avenge our blood? <sup>ⓜ</sup>”
CSB17.11: So they were each given a white robe, and they were told to rest a little while longer until the number would be completed of their fellow servants and their brothers and sisters, who were going to be killed just as they had been.
CSB17.12: Then I saw him open <sup>[8]</sup> the sixth seal. A violent earthquake occurred; <sup>ⓝ</sup> the sun turned black like sackcloth <sup>ⓐ</sup> made of hair; the entire moon <sup>[9]</sup> became like blood; <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.13: the stars <sup>[10]</sup> of heaven fell to the earth as a fig tree drops its unripe figs when shaken by a high wind;
CSB17.14: the sky was split apart like a scroll being rolled up; <sup>ⓒ</sup> and every mountain and island was moved from its place. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.15: Then the kings of the earth, the nobles, the generals, the rich, the powerful, and every slave and free person hid in the caves and among the rocks of the mountains. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.16: And they said to the mountains and to the rocks, “Fall on us and hide us <sup>ⓕ</sup> from the face of the one seated on the throne and from the wrath of the Lamb,
CSB17.17: because the great day of their <sup>[11]</sup> wrath has come! And who is able to stand?” <sup>ⓖ</sup>

AMP.1: Then I saw as the Lamb (Christ) broke one of the seven seals [of the scroll initiating the judgments], and I heard one of the four living creatures call out as with a voice of thunder, " Come."
AMP.2: I looked, and behold, a white horse [of victory] whose rider carried a bow; and a crown [of victory] was given to him, and he rode forth conquering and to conquer.
AMP.3: When He (the Lamb) broke the second seal, I heard the second living creature call out, "Come."
AMP.4: And another, a fiery red horse [of bloodshed], came out; and its rider was empowered to take peace from the earth, so that men would slaughter one another; and a great sword [of war and violent death] was given to him.
AMP.5: When He (the Lamb) broke open the third seal, I heard the third living creature call out, "Come." I looked, and behold, a black horse [of famine]; and the rider had in his hand a pair of scales (a balance).
AMP.6: And I heard something like a voice in the midst of the four living creatures saying, "A quart of wheat for a denarius (a day's wages), and three quarts of barley for a denarius; and do not damage the oil and the wine."
AMP.7: When He (the Lamb) broke open the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature call out, "Come."
AMP.8: So I looked, and behold, an ashen (pale greenish gray) horse [like a corpse, representing death and pestilence]; and its rider's name was Death; and Hades (the realm of the dead) was following with him. They were given authority and power over a fourth part of the earth, to kill with the sword and with famine and with plague (pestilence, disease) and by the wild beasts of the earth.
AMP.9: When He (the Lamb) broke open the fifth seal, I saw underneath the altar the souls of those who had been slaughtered because of the word of God, and because of the testimony which they had maintained [out of loyalty to Christ].
AMP.10: They cried in a loud voice, saying, "O Lord, holy and true, how long now before You will sit in judgment and avenge our blood on those [unregenerate ones] who dwell on the earth?"
AMP.11: Then they were each given a white robe; and they were told to rest and wait quietly for a little while longer, until the number of their fellow servants and their brothers and sisters who were to be killed even as they had been, would be completed.
AMP.12: I looked when He (the Lamb) broke open the sixth seal, and there was a great earthquake; and the sun became black as sackcloth [made] of hair, and the whole moon became like blood;
AMP.13: and the stars of the sky fell to the earth, like a fig tree shedding its late [summer] figs when shaken by a strong wind.
AMP.14: The sky was split [separated from the land] and rolled up like a scroll, and every mountain and island were dislodged and moved out of their places.
AMP.15: Then the kings of the earth and the great men and the military commanders and the wealthy and the strong and everyone, [whether] slave or free, hid themselves in the caves and among the rocks of the mountains;
AMP.16: and they called to the mountains and the rocks, "Fall on us and hide us from the face of Him who sits on the throne, and from the [righteous] wrath and indignation of the Lamb;
AMP.17: for the great day of their wrath and vengeance and retribution has come, and who is able to [face God and] stand [before the wrath of the Lamb]?"

MSG.1: I watched while the Lamb ripped off the first of the seven seals. I heard one of the Animals roar, "Come out!"
MSG.2: I looked—I saw a white horse. Its rider carried a bow and was given a victory garland. He rode off victorious, conquering right and left.
MSG.3: When the Lamb ripped off the second seal, I heard the second Animal cry, "Come out!"
MSG.4: Another horse appeared, this one red. Its rider was off to take peace from the earth, setting people at each other's throats, killing one another. He was given a huge sword.
MSG.5: When he ripped off the third seal, I heard the third Animal cry, "Come out!" I looked. A black horse this time. Its rider carried a set of scales in his hand.
MSG.6: I heard a message (it seemed to issue from the Four Animals): "A quart of wheat for a day's wages, or three quarts of barley, but all the oil and wine you want."
MSG.7: When he ripped off the fourth seal, I heard the fourth Animal cry, "Come out!"
MSG.8: I looked. A colorless horse, sickly pale. Its rider was Death, and Hell was close on its heels. They were given power to destroy a fourth of the earth by war, famine, disease, and wild beasts.
MSG.9: When he ripped off the fifth seal, I saw the souls of those killed because they had held firm in their witness to the Word of God. They were gathered under the Altar,
MSG.10: and cried out in loud prayers, "How long, Strong God, Holy and True? How long before you step in and avenge our murders?"
MSG.11: Then each martyr was given a white robe and told to sit back and wait until the full number of martyrs was filled from among their servant companions and friends in the faith.
MSG.12: I watched while he ripped off the sixth seal: a bone-jarring earthquake, sun turned black as ink, moon all bloody,
MSG.13: stars falling out of the sky like figs shaken from a tree in a high wind,
MSG.14: sky snapped shut like a book, islands and mountains sliding this way and that.
MSG.15: And then pandemonium, everyone and his dog running for cover—kings, princes, generals, rich and strong, along with every commoner, slave or free. They hid in mountain caves and rocky dens,
MSG.16: calling out to mountains and rocks, "Refuge! Hide us from the One Seated on the Throne and the wrath of the Lamb!
MSG.17: The great Day of their wrath has come—who can stand it?"

NIV.1:
The Seals
I watched as the Lamb opened the first of the seven seals. Then I heard one of the four living creatures say in a voice like thunder, “Come!”
NIV.2: I looked, and there before me was a white horse! Its rider held a bow, and he was given a crown, and he rode out as a conqueror bent on conquest.
NIV.3: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, “Come!”
NIV.4: Then another horse came out, a fiery red one. Its rider was given power to take peace from the earth and to make men slay each other. To him was given a large sword.
NIV.5: When the Lamb opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, “Come!” I looked, and there before me was a black horse! Its rider was holding a pair of scales in his hand.
NIV.6: Then I heard what sounded like a voice among the four living creatures, saying, “A quart of wheat for a day’s wages, and three quarts of barley for a day’s wages, and do not damage the oil and the wine!”
NIV.7: When the Lamb opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, “Come!”
NIV.8: I looked, and there before me was a pale horse! Its rider was named Death, and Hades was following close behind him. They were given power over a fourth of the earth to kill by sword, famine and plague, and by the wild beasts of the earth.
NIV.9: When he opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of those who had been slain because of the word of God and the testimony they had maintained.
NIV.10: They called out in a loud voice, “How long, Sovereign Lord, holy and true, until you judge the inhabitants of the earth and avenge our blood?”
NIV.11: Then each of them was given a white robe, and they were told to wait a little longer, until the number of their fellow servants and brothers who were to be killed as they had been was completed.
NIV.12: I watched as he opened the sixth seal. There was a great earthquake. The sun turned black like sackcloth made of goat hair, the whole moon turned blood red,
NIV.13: and the stars in the sky fell to earth, as late figs drop from a fig tree when shaken by a strong wind.
NIV.14: The sky receded like a scroll, rolling up, and every mountain and island was removed from its place.
NIV.15: Then the kings of the earth, the princes, the generals, the rich, the mighty, and every slave and every free man hid in caves and among the rocks of the mountains.
NIV.16: They called to the mountains and the rocks, “Fall on us and hide us from the face of him who sits on the throne and from the wrath of the Lamb!
NIV.17: For the great day of their wrath has come, and who can stand?”

NRSVCE.1: Then I saw the Lamb open one of the seven seals, and I heard one of the four living creatures call out, as with a voice of thunder, “Come!”
NRSVCE.2: I looked, and there was a white horse! Its rider had a bow; a crown was given to him, and he came out conquering and to conquer.
NRSVCE.3: When he opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature call out, “Come!”
NRSVCE.4: And out came another horse, bright red; its rider was permitted to take peace from the earth, so that people would slaughter one another; and he was given a great sword.
NRSVCE.5: When he opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature call out, “Come!” I looked, and there was a black horse! Its rider held a pair of scales in his hand,
NRSVCE.6: and I heard what seemed to be a voice in the midst of the four living creatures saying, “A quart of wheat for a day’s pay, and three quarts of barley for a day’s pay, but do not damage the olive oil and the wine!”
NRSVCE.7: When he opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature call out, “Come!”
NRSVCE.8: I looked and there was a pale green horse! Its rider’s name was Death, and Hades followed with him; they were given authority over a fourth of the earth, to kill with sword, famine, and pestilence, and by the wild animals of the earth.
NRSVCE.9: When he opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of those who had been slaughtered for the word of God and for the testimony they had given;
NRSVCE.10: they cried out with a loud voice, “Sovereign Lord, holy and true, how long will it be before you judge and avenge our blood on the inhabitants of the earth?”
NRSVCE.11: They were each given a white robe and told to rest a little longer, until the number would be complete both of their fellow servants and of their brothers and sisters, who were soon to be killed as they themselves had been killed.
NRSVCE.12: When he opened the sixth seal, I looked, and there came a great earthquake; the sun became black as sackcloth, the full moon became like blood,
NRSVCE.13: and the stars of the sky fell to the earth as the fig tree drops its winter fruit when shaken by a gale.
NRSVCE.14: The sky vanished like a scroll rolling itself up, and every mountain and island was removed from its place.
NRSVCE.15: Then the kings of the earth and the magnates and the generals and the rich and the powerful, and everyone, slave and free, hid in the caves and among the rocks of the mountains,
NRSVCE.16: calling to the mountains and rocks, “Fall on us and hide us from the face of the one seated on the throne and from the wrath of the Lamb;
NRSVCE.17: for the great day of their wrath has come, and who is able to stand?”

NET.1: I looked on when the Lamb opened one of the seven seals, and I heard one of the four living creatures saying with a thunderous voice, “Come!”
NET.2: So I looked, and here came a white horse! The one who rode it had a bow, and he was given a crown, and as a conqueror he rode out to conquer.
NET.3: Then when the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature saying, “Come!”
NET.4: And another horse, fiery red, came out, and the one who rode it was granted permission to take peace from the earth, so that people would butcher one another, and he was given a huge sword.
NET.5: Then when the Lamb opened the third seal I heard the third living creature saying, “Come!” So I looked, and here came a black horse! The one who rode it had a balance scale in his hand.
NET.6: Then I heard something like a voice from among the four living creatures saying, “A quart of wheat will cost a day’s pay and three quarts of barley will cost a day’s pay. But do not damage the olive oil and the wine!”
NET.7: Then when the Lamb opened the fourth seal I heard the voice of the fourth living creature saying, “Come!”
NET.8: So I looked and here came a pale green horse! The name of the one who rode it was Death, and Hades followed right behind. They were given authority over a fourth of the earth, to kill its population with the sword, famine, and disease, and by the wild animals of the earth.
NET.9: Now when the Lamb opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of those who had been violently killed because of the word of God and because of the testimony they had given.
NET.10: They cried out with a loud voice, “How long, Sovereign Master, holy and true, before you judge those who live on the earth and avenge our blood?”
NET.11: Each of them was given a long white robe and they were told to rest for a little longer, until the full number was reached of both their fellow servants and their brothers who were going to be killed just as they had been.
NET.12: Then I looked when the Lamb opened the sixth seal, and a huge earthquake took place; the sun became as black as sackcloth made of hair, and the full moon became blood red;
NET.13: and the stars in the sky fell to the earth like a fig tree dropping its unripe figs when shaken by a fierce wind.
NET.14: The sky was split apart like a scroll being rolled up, and every mountain and island was moved from its place.
NET.15: Then the kings of the earth, the very important people, the generals, the rich, the powerful, and everyone, slave and free, hid themselves in the caves and among the rocks of the mountains.
NET.16: They said to the mountains and to the rocks, “Fall on us and hide us from the face of the one who is seated on the throne and from the wrath of the Lamb,
NET.17: because the great day of their wrath has come, and who is able to withstand it?”
